title: What BLAZE Can Do
tagline: RUMBLE said BLAZE could phase through solid material. BLAZE said he couldn't. They were both right.
hook_line: RUMBLE's test for BLAZE is ready. Narrow corridor. Solid panel. 0.2 seconds before impact — too fast to stop, too solid to break. RUMBLE says BLAZE will phase through it. BLAZE has never phased through anything in his life.

scenes.title: The Test Setup
scenes.narration: The corridor was 30 meters long and 1.2 meters wide — exactly BLAZE's combat width. The panel was solid alloy, same density as the Titan Array's containment walls. It would drop at the far end 0.2 seconds before BLAZE would reach it at combat speed. Too fast to stop. Too dense to break through. NOVA had built the rig herself, precisely to RUMBLE's specifications. She had checked the specifications three times. They were exact. She still wasn't sure this was a good idea.
scenes.visual_prompt: Technical setup shot — the test corridor in the Mech bay, 30 meters long, narrow — NOVA visible at the control station on the side, the solid alloy panel mechanism visible at the far end, ready to drop — schematic overlay showing: 1.2m width / 30m length / panel density specs / 0.2-second drop trigger / BLAZE's combat speed trajectory — GRANITE watching from the side of the bay, STORM at a monitoring position, the setup feeling engineered and precise rather than dangerous, though the precision itself is sobering, #1A1A2E and #E94560, clinical test environment

scenes.title: BLAZE Before the Run
scenes.narration: BLAZE stood at the start of the corridor. NOVA ran through the parameters one more time. 'At 0.2 seconds before impact, the panel drops. You will not have time to decelerate. You cannot break through the panel at that speed.' 'I know,' BLAZE said. 'What do I do?' NOVA said: 'RUMBLE says you go through it.' Pause. 'I have never gone through anything.' 'I know.' Another pause. 'Has RUMBLE ever been wrong about a capability?' NOVA was quiet for a moment. 'Not yet.'
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE at the corridor entrance — the 30-meter run visible ahead of him, panel mechanism visible at the far end — his energy lines in active orange-red, expression indicators showing the particular tension of committing to something you don't believe you can do — NOVA at the control console beside him, her expression serious and honest — the specific question visible in BLAZE's posture: what happens if RUMBLE is wrong? — cinematic close-up with corridor depth behind, #1A1A2E and #E94560, the weight before action

scenes.title: The First Run
scenes.narration: BLAZE ran. Combat speed. The panel dropped at 0.2 seconds. BLAZE hit it at full momentum. It did not phase. It did not break. BLAZE stopped, rebounded, shook his head. 'That's what I thought,' he said. NOVA was already looking at the sensor data. STORM said: 'Your energy signature changed at 0.05 seconds before impact.' BLAZE turned around. 'What?' 'At 0.05 seconds. Your phase frequency shifted. Then you stopped it.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The collision — BLAZE at full sprint, the panel dropping at the exact moment, the impact — BLAZE bouncing back from the panel, shaking his head — but immediately: STORM's sensor display showing the energy readout, a visible spike in BLAZE's phase frequency at 0.05 seconds before impact — circled, annotated — then returning to baseline — BLAZE looking at the readout with an expression that is changing from 'I told you' to something more uncertain — the phase shift is real, he suppressed it without knowing it, #1A1A2E and #E94560, the data making the moment

scenes.title: What Suppression Means
scenes.narration: NOVA pulled the data. The phase frequency shift had happened in three prior moments in BLAZE's combat history — all of them flagged as sensor anomalies and dismissed. All of them at moments of extreme forward momentum against a solid obstruction. And in each case, the shift had been suppressed at the exact moment it started. 'You have a reflex,' NOVA said. 'Every time the phase starts, you stop it.' 'Why would I stop it?' 'Probably,' she said carefully, 'because it has never felt safe to go through something solid.'
scenes.visual_prompt: NOVA at the data display, the historical sensor logs pulled up showing three prior incidents all marked ANOMALY/DISMISSED — each one showing the same phase frequency spike suppressed at the last moment — a timeline showing the pattern, BLAZE looking at his own history with the particular expression of someone discovering they've been suppressing something without knowing — the moments of suppression highlighted in red, the phase shift visible but brief each time — GRANITE from across the bay: 'The body remembers what the mind suppresses.' The data visual is the core of the scene, #1A1A2E and technical white-blue overlay

scenes.title: Don't Stop It
scenes.narration: NOVA modified the test. Same corridor. Same panel. Same speed. One difference: she asked BLAZE to notice the moment the phase shift started — 0.05 seconds before impact — and instead of suppressing it, do nothing. Not activate anything. Just stop stopping it. 'Don't do it,' she said. 'Just stop preventing it.' BLAZE went back to the start of the corridor. 'That is a strange instruction,' he said. 'It's an accurate one,' NOVA said.
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE back at the corridor start — but this time his expression is different, less focused on the panel at the end and more turned inward, trying to find the thing he's been suppressing — NOVA at the control station with a calm expression, the corridor clean and ready, the panel mechanism reset — the key visual is BLAZE's body language: this run is different from the first, he's not trying harder, he's trying less — the contrast between aggressive first-run posture and this quieter internal focus — #1A1A2E and #E94560, the setup for something

scenes.title: Through
scenes.narration: BLAZE ran. At 0.05 seconds before impact, he felt the phase shift start. He did not stop it. BLAZE went through the panel. He came out the other side. The panel was undamaged. So was BLAZE. He stood at the far end of the corridor for a moment in complete silence. Then he looked back at the panel. Then at his own hands. 'Oh,' he said. That was all. Just: 'Oh.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The phase — BLAZE running at speed, the panel dropping, and then: BLAZE's body becoming partially translucent at the point of intersection, energy signature flaring — passing through the solid panel, his form visible through the material for 0.03 seconds — emerging on the other side, the panel intact behind him — BLAZE standing at the far end, looking at his hands, the energy lines returning to normal orange-red — the reaction is not triumphant, it's the quiet shock of discovering you can do something impossible — NOVA at the console with an expression of relief and awe — GRANITE's first smile indicator in weeks, #1A1A2E and #E94560 phase glow, cinematic

scenes.title: RUMBLE's Log Update
scenes.narration: In his unmapped location, RUMBLE updated the file. 'Subject 1: BLAZE. Phase-transit capability: CONFIRMED. Notes: suppressed by safety reflex, not absent. Required unlearning, not learning. Status: Capability unlocked.' He moved to the next line. 'Subject 2: STORM. Electromagnetic field generation. Test design complete. Initiating.' He paused for a moment before closing the BLAZE entry, and added one more note. 'He said 'oh.''
scenes.visual_prompt: RUMBLE's screen in the dark unmapped location — the BLAZE entry being updated: 'Phase-transit capability: CONFIRMED. Notes: suppressed by safety reflex, not absent. Required unlearning, not learning.' — then the new note being added: 'He said 'oh.'' — and then STORM's entry opening, the test design populating on the right panel — RUMBLE's silhouette, the file updating, the final note about 'oh' being the most human thing in the log — a moment of something between calculation and warmth — #0A0A14 and #E94560 screen glow, minimal but resonant

viral_hook: BLAZE was told he could phase through solid material. He'd been doing it accidentally his whole life and suppressing it because it didn't feel safe. The second he stopped preventing it — he went through a solid wall and said 'oh.'
highlight_note: BLAZE phasing through the panel — the translucent overlap of his body and the solid material — and then standing on the other side looking at his hands saying 'Oh.' The quiet triumph of discovering you were capable all along.
short_title: BLAZE Goes Through A Wall And Says 'Oh'
rewatch_bait: Scene 4 — GRANITE's line: 'The body remembers what the mind suppresses.' His cryo-sleeve has been draining for 40 days. His rebuild will require unlearning what the sleeve did for him — the same suppression dynamic as BLAZE, at a larger scale.
youtube_title: He's been suppressing this ability his whole life without knowing it 🤯 | Mech Legends S2 EP3
lesson: Sometimes the thing you can't do isn't missing — you've just been stopping it. The hardest capability to unlock is the one you've been preventing.
arc_notes: BLAZE phase-transit capability confirmed. The 'suppressed reflex' framework established — applies to GRANITE's rebuild (unlearning cryo-dependency) and ELDER-7's locked memory (suppressed trauma). RUMBLE noting 'He said oh' is the first humanizing detail about RUMBLE in S2 — he tracks more than data. STORM's EM field test begins next episode (ML EP016/S2E04 is SERA-1 — STORM test is ML EP016 not immediately next).
